subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the psychological conflict in the poem
Interpret symbolism and irony in Amanda’s fantasies
Examine poetic structure, tone shifts, and literary devices
Evaluate parenting styles and adolescent response
Develop advanced analytical and argumentative writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Contextual and Psychological Framework
teaching_strategy: Discuss generation gap and emotional development
student_activities: Write brief reflection on freedom vs discipline
assessment: Review written reflections for depth of thought

lesson_plan.title: Close Reading and Structural Analysis
topics_covered: Effect of parentheses and stanza structure
student_activities: Annotate the poem for tone shifts
Write a short analytical note on structure

lesson_plan.title: Symbolism, Irony, and Literary Devices
topics_covered: Symbolic interpretation of mermaid, orphan, Rapunzel
teaching_strategy: Guide symbolic interpretation with textual evidence
Discuss ironic implications of isolation in fantasies
student_activities: Identify and explain three literary devices with examples
assessment: Evaluate written analysis for interpretation depth
Check correctness of literary device identification

lesson_plan.title: Character Study and Critical Interpretation
topics_covered: Impact of repeated criticism on self-esteem
teaching_strategy: Relate poem to modern parenting challenges
student_activities: Write a comparative character analysis (Amanda vs Parent)
Present arguments defending or criticizing the parent
assessment: Assess comparative analysis for balance and evidence
Evaluate clarity of arguments in presentation

lesson_plan.title: Comprehensive Literary Appreciation
topics_covered: Theme, tone, structure, and message synthesis
teaching_strategy: Provide structure for literary appreciation essay
student_activities: Write a 200–250 word critical appreciation of the poem
Compose a creative rewriting from the parent’s perspective
assessment: Evaluate essay for organization, interpretation, and textual support
Assess creativity and coherence in rewriting task

teaching_aids: Presentation slides on symbolism and irony
teacher_tip: Encourage higher-order thinking by asking 'why' and 'how' questions. Push students beyond summary toward interpretation, symbolism, and psychological insight while maintaining sensitivity to personal experiences.
